title: Project Creation & Import Wizard
description: As a user, I want a guided, multi-step wizard when adding a project so that I can seamlessly create new projects with local file structures and remote repositories, or easily import existing ones.

features.title: Step 1 - Initialization Type
features.description: A launch screen for the wizard that gives the user two main paths: Create a New Project or Import an Existing Project.

features.title: Step 2 - Project Details & FS Setup
features.description: A configuration screen for brand new projects to define basic metadata and generate the local filesystem structure.

features.title: Step 3 - Source Control Integration
features.description: A final setup screen that handles Git initialization and remote repository linking (GitHub/Bitbucket).

features.title: Step 2b - Import Existing Project Screen
features.description:
A dedicated import flow within the wizard that allows users to bring an existing project into Overseer Local through three supported import modes: (1) importing a `project.json` file that contains the full project definition, (2) importing an existing local folder as a local-only project, and (3) importing an existing local folder where a Git repository is detected and repository details can be pre-filled automatically.

Acceptance criteria:
- The import path is a first-class branch of the project wizard, separate from the new-project creation path.
- The user can choose between `project.json` import and local-folder import.
- Selecting a `project.json` file reads the project definition and pre-fills all supported project details in the wizard.
- Invalid, unreadable, or incomplete `project.json` files surface clear validation errors and prevent completion until resolved.
- Selecting a local folder validates that the path exists and is readable.
- If the selected local folder does not contain Git metadata, the project can still be imported as a local-only project.
- If the selected local folder contains a detectable Git repository, the wizard identifies it automatically and pre-fills relevant repository information.
- The user can review and edit imported or detected values before final confirmation.
- The final confirmation step clearly shows which import mode is being used and what local/Git metadata will be saved.
- Error states for invalid paths, unreadable folders, malformed project definitions, and failed Git detection are actionable and non-destructive.
